title: Lesson 29: Introduction to GitHub (Saving Your Code Like a Developer)

content.explanation:
🌐 Welcome to Real Development Workflow

Until now, you have been writing code inside a browser-based editor.

Real developers do something different — they store and manage code using GitHub.

📦 What is GitHub?

GitHub is a platform where developers:

  • Save code safely
  • Track changes
  • Work on projects together
  • Deploy websites

⚡ Why It Matters

👉 Every professional website project lives on GitHub before deployment.

syntax_breakdown.content:
🧪 Basic GitHub Workflow

1. Create a repository (project)
2. Upload your HTML files
3. Commit changes (save versions)
4. Push updates to GitHub

📌 Key Terms

  • Repo = project folder on GitHub
  • Commit = saved version of code
  • Push = upload code online

🧠 Key Idea

👉 GitHub = Google Drive for developers (but smarter)

editor_sandbox.starter_code:
My Developer Journey

I am learning how to store and manage code like a professional developer.

mini_challenge.instruction:
🎯 Mission: Prepare your project for GitHub

1. Add a heading called 'My First Repository'
2. Add a paragraph explaining what your project is
3. Add a list of files you would upload:
   - index.html
   - about.html
   - images folder

🔥 Think like you're preparing a real GitHub project

quiz_engine.question: What is GitHub mainly used for?
quiz_engine.explanation_feedback: Correct 🎉 GitHub is used to store, manage, and collaborate on code projects.

takeaways: Developers use it to track changes
GitHub is essential for real-world development
